title: Build swift-http-traffic-capture-cli — Phase B (hardest engineering) of scaffold-app-from-web pipeline
description: Substrate-native HTTP traffic capture in pure Swift. Embedded WKWebView with custom WKURLSchemeHandler OR URLProtocol subclass intercepts all HTTP requests + responses during page interaction. Operator-script-driven: --interaction-script flag accepts a JSON file describing user flow (login fill, click selectors, navigation steps). Captured traffic emitted as HAR-like JSON in captures/api/traffic.json. Headers + auth tokens + cookies cataloged separately (auth.json) for downstream codegen to know what auth model to scaffold. NO mitmproxy, NO Charles, NO Playwright sidecar — pure WebKit + Foundation URLSession. This is likely the hardest engineering of the 7 phases: WKWebView's URL scheme handler only intercepts custom schemes by default; for http/https, need URLProtocol-based interception OR network extension. Research path: investigate WKWebView + URLProtocol interop; fall back to NWConnection + man-in-the-middle approach if URLProtocol can't reach WKWebView's underlying session.
